label: Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, no hall — ensuite sandwiched between master (front) and standard (rear) in the bedroom column, mirroring the squarish distributed-bath pattern (1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_ds_gr). Both bedrooms door directly into the great room; common bath gets its own direct doors from standard (private route) and from the great room (guest-accessible route) instead of routing through a hall.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — door on master's north wall, ensuite sits directly behind master in the bedroom column's mid band. Same distributed-bath idiom as the squarish ds catalog, brought into the wide shell.
ensuite's north wall touches standard's south wall — no door, acoustic+privacy buffer between the two bedrooms.
master's east wall = great room's west wall in the front band — a real door, no hall buffer.

standard's east wall = great room's west wall in the rear band — a real door, no hall buffer. With the hall removed, both bedrooms access the great room directly, same as the squarish distributed-bath sibling.

declared for adjacency/geometry purposes (pulls common next to standard) but architectural_plan.py's generic bedroom<->common-bath rule always suppresses this into a wall, never a door — common baths are never directly bedroom-accessible. Same no-op declaration exists on the squarish ds_gr sibling. Standard's only route to common is via the great room door below.

common bath's ONLY actual door — opens onto the great room, mirrors the squarish distributed-bath sibling's common↔great door. With the hall removed and the standard↔common door suppressed (see above), this is the sole way in or out of common bath.
shared plumbing wall — common bath and kitchen stack at the rear.

great_room + kitchen form one open LDK (gr style) — consistent with every wide-shell topology in the catalog so far; the shell's shallower depth doesn't comfortably support a separate living+dining ('ld') split.

soft_proximities.note: pull common toward standard's east wall (the primary bedroom-side user) when there's slack — same idiom used in the squarish distributed-bath topologies.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_side_split_baths_ds_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: On compact wide lots (<80 sqm buildable), kitchen floors at 1.8 m depth (usable galley, above the PD 1096 1.5 m hard min). Both baths get an auto floor so neither collapses to its hard minimum by default; master is capped at 18 sqm so its priority weight doesn't steal depth from the ensuite that's sandwiched behind it. preferred_apply bumps ensuite to 3.0 sqm and kitchen to a more generous 2.0 m galley where the envelope allows — tried first, relaxed together on infeasibility.

notes: Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, no hall. Sibling of 1s_2br_wd_side_split_baths_ds_hall_gr with the hall removed: both bedrooms door directly into the great room (same direct-access idiom master already used), and common bath picks up a second direct door onto the great room instead of routing through hall. Mirrors the squarish 1s_2br_sq_side_split_baths_ds_gr pattern, brought into the wide shell.
Floor band: roughly 65-85 sqm buildable — same territory as the hall variant and side_split_baths_cl_gr, sized against 14x11 to 15x12 m lots.
Front band:  [master            |   GREAT ROOM (direct doors from both bedrooms)  ]
Two sub-columns (no separate mid/hall column, unlike the hall variant):
- LEFT  (~3-3.5 m): [master front, ensuite mid, standard rear] — bedroom column, same distributed stack as squarish ds_gr/ds_ld
- RIGHT (~5.5-6.5 m): [great_room front+mid, kitchen rear] — LDK column, with common bath tucked at the rear boundary next to kitchen
Master and standard access: both door directly into the great room — no hall buffer for either bedroom.

Common bath access: ONE actual door, from the great room — the standard<->common adjacency above is declared (for geometry pull) but always gets suppressed to a wall by architectural_plan.py's generic bedroom<->common-bath rule, same as the squarish ds_gr sibling. So standard's route to the bath is through the great room, same as everyone else's.

Wet stack: common bath's east wall = kitchen's west wall (wet_core). Combined with the rear-setback dirty kitchen, all wet utilities land in one rear-east bay, same as every other topology in the catalog.

Carport: not declared in the base topology — NCP form, consistent with the other wide topologies. To add a side carport, add a building_void at front_right. For a front-parallel carport, brief.carport_side='front' bumps the front setback to 3 m. When brief.carport_side='left', the runner mirrors the topology so the LDK ends up on the left and the bedroom column on the right.
No fallback_topology declared — matches the squarish ds_gr sibling, which also omits one.
